title: Smoky Ginger Thai Stir-fry
description: A solo-dinner thai dish featuring ginger paste and fold with a smoky flavor, perfect for those looking for a paleo-friendly option.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup almond flour or coconut flour

instructions: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Place in a baking dish coated with coconut oil.
Roast for about 20 minutes or until cooked through. Let cool slightly.
In a large wok or skillet, heat coconut oil over medium-high heat. Add sliced onions and garlic; stir-fry for about 5 minutes.
Add roasted chicken to the pan, along with fish sauce and ginger. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until fragrant and well combined.
To serve, mix almond flour or coconut flour with smoked paprika in a small bowl. Sprinkle over the dish before serving to enhance flavor and texture.
Top with fresh Thai basil leaves and baby spinach just before serving.
